confirmed.note: AKP05 backend scaffolded by cloning the just-written AKP03 backend: buildCmdHeader is byte-identical (empty diff), buildSetBrightness/buildClearAll/buildClearKey/buildImageHeader and the Device class poll()/switch are copied with namespace akp03->akp05 and tweaked constants. Protocol .hpp (akp03_protocol.hpp->akp05_protocol.hpp) and the Catch2 test file are also clones with edited geometry/command words. Same commit, same author, one feed.

Identical IClockCapable::setTime() stub copy-pasted into all 4 Stream Dock backends in one commit: only the once_flag name (s_warned_akpNN) and the log tag (streamdeck.akpNN) differ, body otherwise verbatim (call_once + AJAZZ_LOG_WARN + return NotImplemented). The commit message itself enumerates the 4 near-identical copies. Cries out for a CRTP/base-class helper.

loadTrustRoots() mini-JSON walk is duplicated verbatim (minus comments) between the POSIX and Win32 verifier translation units; the same robustness fix (bound the key->name window by the closing '}') is hand-applied to both copies in this commit. The code comment literally says 'duplicates the POSIX backend logic. Mirroring is intentional'. Real cross-file duplication kept in sync by hand -- exactly the drift risk archcheck targets.

false_positives.what_happened: Checker hint flagged this Qt application.cpp self-similarity, but the commit is a Merge (docs/release-and-workflow); the flagged regions are repeated Qt boilerplate: a sequence of QObject::connect(...) wirings and a QML property-exposure block (qmlRegisterType / setContextProperty rows). Each row is structurally identical (connect(a, &A::sig, b, [this]{...})), so a token detector sees a high-weight near-clone, but it is the canonical Qt signal/slot idiom, not transplanted logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add an idiom-suppression filter for runs of Qt connect()/qmlRegister*/setContextProperty/setProperty statements -- treat consecutive single-statement member-call lines that differ only in argument identifiers as a 'wiring table' and down-weight (same family as data-table suppression). Also skip merge commits when attributing copy-paste hits to a commit (attribute to the introducing non-merge commit via blame).
